frameworks.name: thalamocortical circuit development
frameworks.one_line: MD-PFC loop assembly across embryonic and postnatal stages.

frameworks.name: activity-dependent circuit refinement
frameworks.one_line: Postnatal refinement establishes adult thalamocortical topography.

core_question: How does the mediodorsal thalamus to prefrontal cortex circuit develop, and how do disruptions at distinct developmental stages give rise to shared cognitive deficits?
key_assumptions: MD serves as a fundamental subcortical relay to PFC modulating cognition
MD-PFC development follows highly conserved embryonic and postnatal stages
common cognitive endophenotypes can emerge from disruption at distinct developmental stages
key_findings: embryonic MD undergoes a large neurogenic wave followed by axonal pathfinding into developing PFC
postnatal MD-PFC circuit undergoes activity-dependent refinement establishing adult topography
disruption across distinct developmental stages converges on working-memory and attentional-flexibility deficits in mouse models
interpretations: shared cognitive endophenotypes across diverse developmental perturbations imply convergent MD-PFC circuit vulnerability
MD-PFC circuit dysregulation across staged development can have detrimental cognitive consequences
